title: [InstCombine] Infer nuw for gep inbounds from base of object

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary emphasizes 'single variable index' from original test case, but this uses a constant non-zero index (5). The patch applies to any non-zero index regardless of whether it's variable or constant, which is not explicitly covered in the summary description.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@GlobalArray = global 64 bytes, align 4

define i32 @test_constant_nonzero_index() {
#0:
  %gep = gep inbounds ptr @GlobalArray, 64 x i64 0, 4 x i64 5
  %load = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  ret i32 %load
}
=>
@GlobalArray = global 64 bytes, align 4

define i32 @test_constant_nonzero_index() {
#0:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @GlobalArray, 1 x i64 20
  %load = load i32, ptr %__constexpr_0, align 4
  ret i32 %load
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: All known test cases use variable indices (%idx, %conv.i12, etc.). This tests constant non-zero index (i32 5) which still satisfies 'exactly one non-zero index' condition but represents a different pattern not covered in examples.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
@global_arr = global 128 bytes, align 4

define i32 @constant_nonzero_index() {
#0:
  %gep = gep inbounds ptr @global_arr, 128 x i32 0, 4 x i32 5
  %load = load i32, ptr %gep, align 4
  ret i32 %load
}
=>
@global_arr = global 128 bytes, align 4

define i32 @constant_nonzero_index() {
#0:
  %__constexpr_0 = gep inbounds ptr @global_arr, 1 x i64 20
  %load = load i32, ptr %__constexpr_0, align 4
  ret i32 %load
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:64:64-p2:32:32-p3:32:32-p4:64:64-p5:32:32-p6:32:32-p7:160:256:256:32-p8:128:128-i64:64-v16:16-v24:32-v32:32-v48:64-v96:128-v192:256-v256:256-v512:512-v1024:1024-v2048:2048-n32:64-S32-A5"

define void @gep_in_loop_with_phi_idx(i32 %n) {
  %alloca = alloca [64 x i32], align 4
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %0 ], [ %next, %loop.body ]
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.body, label %loop.exit

loop.body:
  %conv = sext i32 %i to i64
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds [64 x i32], ptr %alloca, i64 0, i64 %conv
  store i32 %i, ptr %gep, align 4
  %next = add i32 %i, 1
  br label %loop.header

loop.exit:
  ret void
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:64:64-p2:32:32-p3:32:32-p4:64:64-p5:32:32-p6:32:32-p7:160:256:256:32-p8:128:128-i64:64-v16:16-v24:32-v32:32-v48:64-v96:128-v192:256-v256:256-v512:512-v1024:1024-v2048:2048-n32:64-S32-A5"

define void @gep_in_loop_with_phi_idx(i32 %n) {
  %alloca = alloca [64 x i32], align 4
  br label %loop.header

loop.header:
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %0 ], [ %next, %loop.body ]
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.body, label %loop.exit

loop.body:
  %conv = sext i32 %i to i64
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds nuw [64 x i32], ptr %alloca, i64 0, i64 %conv
  store i32 %i, ptr %gep, align 4
  %next = add i32 %i, 1
  br label %loop.header

loop.exit:
  ret void
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention control flow context. This test places the GEP inside a loop with a phi node-derived index, demonstrating the optimization applies even in iterative contexts with SSA values from phi nodes, which is not shown in existing tests.

  br label %loop.header

loop.header:

loop.body:

  br label %loop.header

loop.header:                                      ; preds = %loop.body, %0
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %0 ], [ %next, %loop.body ]
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %i, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop.body, label %loop.exit

loop.body:                                        ; preds = %loop.header
  %next = add i32 %i, 1
  br label %loop.header

loop.exit:                                        ; preds = %loop.header
  ret void
}
